title: schoenus pygmaeus
intro: Schoenus pygmaeus, also known as the Dwarf Schoenus or Small Club Sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Schoenus pygmaeus is traditionally prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and poultices, allowing its medicinal properties to be harnessed for various health purposes.
